caption:
Comment “100” if you want a free personalized $100M scaling roadmap for your business in under 30 seconds

Here’s how I got here…

21: Graduated Vanderbilt in 3 years Magna Cum Laude, and took a fancy consulting job.

23 yrs old: Left my fancy consulting job to start a business (a gym).

24 yrs old: Opened 5 gym locations.

26 yrs old: Closed down 6th gym. Lost everything.

26 yrs old: Got back to launching gyms (launched 33). Then, lost everything for a 2nd time.

26 yrs old: In desperation, started licensing model as a hail mary. It worked.

27 yrs old: “Gym Launch” does $3M profit the next 6 months. Then $17M profit next 12 months.

28 yrs old: Started Prestige Labs. $20M the first year.

29 yrs old: Launched ALAN, a software company for agencies to work leads for customers. Scaled to $1.7mmo within 6 months.

31 yrs old: Sold 75% of UseAlan to a strategic buyer in an all stock deal.

31 yrs old: Sold 66% of Gym Launch & Prestige Labs at $46.2M valuation in all-cash deal to American Pacific Group. (you can google it)

31 yrs old: Started our family office Acquisition.com. We invest and scale companies using the $42M in distributions we had taken + the cash from the $46.2M exit.

32 yrs old: Started making free content showing how we grow companies to make real business education accessible to everyone (and) to attract business owners to invest or scale their businesses.

34 yrs old: I became co-founder of Skool - which is a platform for people to build communities online… making a living doing what they love, with people like them.

36 yrs old: I did a $106M book launch selling 3.6M copies, of my $100M Money Models book, in 72 hours, breaking the Guinness world record for the fastest selling, non-fiction book… of all time.

Today: Our portfolio now does $250M/yr in aggregate revenue each year.

To all the gladiators in the arena, we’re all in the middle of writing our own stories. The worse the monsters, the more epic the story.

You either get an epic outcome or an epic story. Both mean you win.

Keep crushing. May your desires be greater than your obstacles.

Never quit
firstComment: 100 Thank you!

caption:
Good talent takes twice as long time find, three times as much to pay, and make you ten times more than you expected.

Hard, expensive, and…worth it.
firstComment: The wrong person in a seat costs more than the right person ever will. The math always works out the same way.

latestComments.text: The wrong person in a seat costs more than the right person ever will. The math always works out the same way.
The real cost of cheap talent is invisible until it compounds. Same with AI — invest early or pay more to catch up later.
The fact that you're at a 78% profit margin leads me to believe that your commission is way too low so good sells people are going to other companies with higher commissions.
He’s Always got the best advice

Learned this the expensive way. Tried to save on talent across three of our companies early on. Turnover cost us more than the premium ever would have. Once we started paying above market for the right people, retention went up and so did output. Cheaper is always more expensive when it comes to people.
The right people feel expensive upfront, but they pay you back in ways average never will.
Happy Easter ✝️🤜🏼🤛🏼 #blessed #grateful🙏 #attitudeofgratitude💫 thanks for sharing this , I hope you have a great day

caption:
You’ll get a lot further once you realize:

Patience
Confidence
Joy
Humility
Discipline
Perseverance
Kindness
Hope
Generosity
Compassion

Aren’t traits you’re born with, but skills you learn.

And you learn them by practicing them everyday.

If a child can learn it, so can you.
firstComment:
Truthfully so much of entrepreneurship is navigating decisions solo and bringing your vision to life in the tangible world in a way that feels meaningful and true for you that also creates impact that naturally evolves over time. 

Numbness often happens when we’re overloaded, undersupported or quite literally paying for success with our own health. 

A solid support system from people who actually see you and encourage you chasing your dreams is also imperative. So much to unpack on his question and obviously love how measurable this response is also. 

So many times I’ve caught myself chasing the wrong numbers in my business and that is definitely what will drain your soul if you’re not careful.
